mechanism_narrative: Low nominal short-term interest rates reduce the equity discount rate and boost corporate net present value, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are not significantly positive (mean <= 0 or t-test p > 0.05) during low FEDFUNDS regimes over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 6.5% indicates significant labor market slack, which suppresses wage inflation and supports corporate profit margins, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: When UNRATE > 6.5%, positive 60-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in retail sales and housing starts generates a joint demand impulse that outperforms either series alone, predicting positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction term of RSAFS and HOUST shows no incremental predictive power (F-test p > 0.05) over a 2-year rolling window, or single-series models outperform the joint model by more than 2% annualized return.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansions, capacity utilization pressures captured by industrial production peaks cause input cost inflation that compresses earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: During late-cycle regimes, INDPRO growth does not negatively predict 60-day returns (Rank IC > 0 or t-stat of negative slope > -1.5) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid 3-month increase in the federal funds rate of more than 0.15 percentage points signals an abrupt monetary tightening that raises discount rates faster than earnings growth, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Following a 0.15pp 3-month rise in FEDFUNDS, SPX 5-day forward returns are not significantly negative (mean >= 0 or t-test p > 0.05) over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month employment growth in nonfarm payrolls reflects rising aggregate income and consumption demand, which drives higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: PAYEMS month-over-month changes show a Rank IC below 0.03 with 5-day SPX forward returns over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, elevated retail sales combined with rising interest rates reflect demand strength that outpaces higher discount rates, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 1.0% and rising over past 3 months
failure_condition: In tightening cycles, the joint signal of rising FEDFUNDS and elevated RSAFS fails to produce positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of months, or the strategy Sharpe ratio falls below 0.3 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production concurrent with a falling unemployment rate confirms a demand-driven expansion where output growth translates into earnings rather than margin compression, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint condition of rising INDPRO and falling UNRATE yields a 60-day forward return Rank IC below 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.
